items.q: Why do earthquakes typically cause more deaths in LICs than HICs?
items.correct: LICs often have poorly enforced building codes, weak emergency response, dense informal housing and limited preparedness — not because the earthquakes are stronger.
items.misconception: LICs have more earthquake deaths because more earthquakes happen there.
items.random_wrong: HICs never have earthquake deaths because of advanced technology.
items.why: Vulnerability is socio-economic, not seismic. Japan and Haiti both sit on plate boundaries; the death toll difference comes from building quality, planning and response — a key development comparison.

items.q: How are fold mountains like the Himalayas formed?
items.correct: By the collision of two continental plates (India and Eurasia) where neither can subduct, so the crust crumples and is forced upwards over millions of years.
items.misconception: Fold mountains form when one plate slides under another into the mantle.
items.random_wrong: Fold mountains form from giant volcanoes erupting upwards.
items.why: Students confuse continent-continent collision with subduction. Continental crust is too buoyant to subduct, so it crumples — distinct from oceanic-continental boundaries like the Andes.

items.q: Why are tropical rainforest soils typically infertile?
items.correct: Heavy rainfall leaches nutrients, and most nutrients are stored in living biomass and rapidly recycled — the soil itself holds very little.
items.misconception: Because so much vegetation grows there, the soil must be fertile.
items.random_wrong: Because rainforest soils are made of clay.
items.why: The lush vegetation hides poor soil. The rainforest works as a tightly closed nutrient cycle; once cleared, the system collapses quickly because the soil cannot sustain growth.

items.q: What is the difference between magma and lava?
items.correct: Magma is below ground, lava is above
items.misconception: Magma is cold, lava is hot
items.random_wrong: Magma is water, lava is rock
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Magma is below ground, lava is above. “Magma is cold, lava is hot” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Plate boundaries. “Middle of oceans only” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Roughly what % of volcanoes are in the Pacific Ring of Fire?
items.why: The real figure is 75. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
